string.text: The Swedish Krag–Jørgensen rifle, adopted in 1894, is a unique bolt-action rifle known for its innovative side-loading magazine and smooth action. Developed by Ole Herman Johannes Krag and Erik Jørgensen, the rifle was used primarily by Swedish forces through the early 20th century. It fired a 6.5x55mm cartridge, which was relatively advanced for the time, providing excellent accuracy and manageable recoil. The side-mounted magazine allowed soldiers to top off rounds without opening the bolt, a feature that gave it a distinctive edge in prolonged engagements. Though eventually phased out in favor of more modern rifles, the Swedish Krag remains celebrated for its craftsmanship and historic significance.\n \n
